text.title: Email Writing Rules and Metrics
text.summary: Per-email structural rules, subject line constraints, CTA policy, and performance targets per email.

rule.statement: Each onboarding email must contain exactly one CTA — one button or one link, never both.
rule.rationale: Multiple CTAs split attention and reduce completion. The single-action constraint forces clarity about the email's purpose.

rule.statement: Write body copy at 3-5 sentences maximum in short paragraphs (1-2 sentences each).
rule.rationale: Onboarding emails compete with product notifications; walls of text get skipped. Short paragraphs signal respect for user time.

rule.statement: Write from a named person (founder or CS rep), not from a company account, during the onboarding phase.
rule.rationale: Personal tone outperforms branded templates in onboarding; plain text often outperforms HTML for the first 14 days.

list.item: Preview text: 40-60 chars, enhances open rate
CTA: one action, button or link (not both)
PS: optional secondary info (reply invitation works well here)

rule.statement: Track email-received-to-action-completed conversion per email, not just open or click rate.
rule.rationale: Open and click metrics measure email quality; the activation conversion metric measures whether the email achieves its actual goal.

list.item: Open rate target: 50%+ (below → fix subject line)
Click rate target: 10%+ (below → fix CTA or copy)
Conversion target: 5%+ (below → reduce friction in product)

rule.statement: A/B test subject lines in the first 4 hours post-send; do not run subject line tests on emails older than 24 hours.
rule.rationale: Onboarding email recency is critical — stale tests are not representative of how current subscribers respond to the trigger context.

section.title: ESP Integration Notes

rule.statement: Specify the ESP (Customer.io, ConvertKit, ActiveCampaign) in every agent prompt producing email copy — personalization token syntax varies by platform.
rule.rationale: Customer.io uses {{customer.first_name}}; ConvertKit uses {{subscriber.first_name}}. Wrong tokens produce broken personalization silently.

section.note: Trigger conditions described in natural language must be translated into ESP-specific automation logic by a human — never deploy agent-described conditions directly to an ESP without human configuration.

text.reference: Welcome email template — first action, reply-friendly
